content.problemStatement:
A particular month has 303030 days, numbered from 111 to 303030.

Day 111 is a Monday, and the usual 777-day week is followed (so day 222 is Tuesday, day 333 is Wednesday, and so on).

Every Saturday and Sunday is a holiday. There are NNN festival days, which are also holidays. Note that it is possible for a festival day to occur on a Saturday or Sunday.

You are given the dates of the festivals. Determine the total number of holidays in this month.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the number of festival days.
The second line of each test case contains NNN **distinct** space-separated integers A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​, denoting the festival days. Note that the AiA_iAi​ are **not necessarily given in sorted order.**


Output Format
For each test case, output a new line containing the total number of holidays.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the number of festival days.
- The second line contains N distinct integers A1 A2 … AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 30) denoting the festival days. If N = 0 the second line may be empty.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total number of holidays in the month.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Weekend days are {6,7,13,14,20,21,27,28}.
Test case 1: festivals {5,7}. Day 7 is already a weekend, only day 5 adds a new holiday → 8+1=9.
Test case 2: festivals {23,1,6}. Day 6 is a weekend, days 1 and 23 are new → 8+2=10.
Test case 3: festival {13} is a weekend, no new holidays → 8.

hidden.label: almost all weekends plus one extra

solution.approach: The month has exactly 30 days. Day 1 is Monday, therefore the Saturdays are days 6,13,20,27 and the Sundays are days 7,14,21,28. These eight days are always holidays. For each festival day we check whether it already belongs to the weekend set. If it does not, it adds one new holiday. The answer for a test case is 8 + (number of festival days that are not weekends). The check can be done with a hash set or a boolean array of size 31.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of festival days (at most 30).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a small fixed set of weekend days is stored.
